LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RONALD INDECH'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS APPEAR TO LACK MERIT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM APPEARS TO BE A CONTRACT DISPUTE. SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY, HOWEVER, ARE NOT WARRANTED UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7. WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED INJURIES RISE TO THE LEVEL OF SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS NOT AN ISSUE, IN THIS INSTANCE, THAT SHOULD BE RESOLVED ON A MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION RECLASSIFIES THIS CASE AS A SMALL CLAIMS COURT ACTION. THE ACTION IS TRANSFERED TO THE SMALL CLAIMS DEPARTMENT OF THIS COURT PURSUANT TO CCP 403.040 (A). NO FURTHER FILING FEES ARE REQUIRED FROM PLAINTIFF AND NO FURTHER SERVICE OF THE COMPLAINT IS REQUIRED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ON CALENDAR FOR FEBRUARY 24, 2016 ARE OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Defendant Ronald Indech'S Motion For Imposition Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Reyman of Dental & Medical Counsel, PC telephone number 925-999-8200 for Plaintiff.
Kyle Schriner of Schriner Law Firm, PC telephone number 415-321-4924 for and with Defendant Ronald Indech.

Plaintiff's Counsel indicated that he did not have authority to stipulate to reclassification of the matter to Small Claims Court. The Court modified its tentative ruling. Defendant Ronald Indech's Motion For Imposition Of Sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress appear to lack merit. Plaintiff's claim appears to be a contract dispute. Sanctions against Plaintiff and his attorney, however, are not warranted under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7. Whether Plaintiff's alleged injuries rise to the level of severe emotional distress is not an issue, in this instance, that should be resolved on a motion for sanctions. The Court on its own motion reclassifies this case as a Small Claims Court action. The action is transfered to the Small Claims Department of this Court pursuant to CCP 403.040 (a). No further filing fees are required from Plaintiff and no further service of the complaint is required. Order signed in open Court. The motion to strike second amended complaint and the demurrer to second amended complaint on calendar for February 24, 2016 are off calendar.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ALI OROMCHIAN'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. There is a strong policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings. Defendant has not demonstrated that the proposed second amended complaint is a sham pleading or that it will be prejudiced by the granting of the motion. The case is still in the early stages of discovery, a trial date has not been set, and the first case management conference has yet to be held. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-23-2015 Continued To Feb-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion: (1) To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Special Interrogatory, And Form Interrogatories, And (2) For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KYLE L. SCHRINER, ESQ. of Schriner Law Firm, PC, Tel No. 415-321-4924 appearing for Defendant Ronald Indech;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT RONALD INDECH'S Motion: (1) To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Special Interrogatory, And Form Interrogatories, And (2) For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney, the Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admissions 5 and 7 are denied. Motion regarding RFAs 1, 3 and 4 is granted in that Plaintiff must clarify his responses by identifying who he paid the money to and/or who he received the tickets from. The Motion to Compel further responses to Special Interrogatory 4 is denied. The Motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory 150.1 is granted in that Plaintiff's later responses state that there was no written contract between the parties. Therefore, it is presumed that that the proper response to 150.1 is "none." The Motion is denied as to Form Interrogatories 150.2 and 150.3. No sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
